intro:
There is limited information about the Podospermum columnae plant. However, I found that Podospermum is a genus of plants in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and Podospermum is a type of composite plant. 

A more commonly known plant in the same genus is Podospermum (Podospermum laciniatum) which is a small annual herb native to the southwestern part of the Western Cape province of South Africa. 

Podospermum laciniatum has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and as a purgative. The bioactive constituents of Podospermum laciniatum include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. 

The most common herbal preparations made with Podospermum laciniatum include infusions and decoctions, which are used to treat various ailments.
